name: First Cash Pawn
description_short: FirstCash is the leading international pawn shop operator with 3,300+ locations across 29 U.S. states, the UK, and Latin America, offering pawn loans secured by personal property and retail merchandise sales.
description_long:
FirstCash, Inc. is headquartered in Fort Worth, Texas and operates as the leading international pawn shop chain with over 3,300 retail locations. The company was founded to serve cash and credit-constrained consumers through a network spanning 29 U.S. states, Washington D.C., the United Kingdom, and Latin America (Mexico, Guatemala, Colombia, and El Salvador). FirstCash is a publicly traded company listed in both the S&P MidCap 400 Index and Russell 2000 Index, employing approximately 22,000 people globally.

FirstCash's core business model centers on non-recourse pawn loans secured by pledged personal property, meaning borrowers are not liable for repayment beyond losing their collateral. The company buys and sells a wide variety of merchandise including jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sporting goods, and musical instruments. Additionally, FirstCash operates layaway services with 10% down payment options for customers seeking installment purchasing, and offers gold and precious metal buying services for immediate cash needs.

What distinguishes FirstCash from smaller pawn operators is its massive scale—3,300+ locations—combined with technology infrastructure. The company also operates AFF, a subsidiary providing lease-to-own and retail finance payment solutions through 15,000+ merchant partner locations nationwide, demonstrating diversification beyond traditional pawn lending. This dual-business model positions FirstCash as both a pawn lender and fintech-adjacent player in consumer finance.

FirstCash is best suited for consumers experiencing temporary cash constraints who have valuable personal property to pledge. The honest caveat is that pawn loans come with high implied interest rates (typically 10-25% monthly on annualized basis), making them expensive credit compared to traditional loans. Additionally, borrowers risk losing sentimental or valuable items if unable to repay, and the pawn model inherently serves only those with physical collateral.
pros: Massive network of 3,300+ locations provides convenient access across 29 U.S. states, UK, and Latin America
Non-recourse pawn loans mean no personal liability beyond loss of collateral—borrowers cannot be sued for deficiency
Same-day cash available for pledged items with no credit check required
Wide variety of merchandise accepted (jewelry, electronics, tools, instruments, appliances, sporting goods)
Layaway service available with only 10% down payment, reducing upfront cost for purchases
Gold and precious metal buying service for quick cash conversion
Transparent pawn loan process with no hidden fees (standard in pawn industry)
cons: High implied interest rates on pawn loans (typically 10-25% monthly annualized) make them significantly more expensive than traditional personal loans
Risk of losing sentimental or valuable personal property if unable to repay within the loan term
Limited to consumers with valuable physical collateral—not accessible to those without items to pledge
Inventory of used merchandise varies by location; finding specific desired items not guaranteed
No credit building benefit—pawn loans do not report to credit bureaus or help establish credit history
best_for: Consumers with temporary cash needs who own valuable items (jewelry, electronics, instruments) and lack traditional credit access
Individuals seeking emergency funding without credit checks or application processes
People with poor credit seeking quick liquidity without loan rejection risk
Budget-conscious shoppers interested in purchasing used merchandise or layaway plans
diagnosis: FirstCash is best for consumers facing temporary cash emergencies who own valuable personal property and cannot access traditional credit due to poor credit history or lack of time. The critical caveat is that pawn loans carry very high effective interest rates (10-25% monthly) and risk permanent loss of collateral if repayment is missed, making them suitable only for short-term bridge financing, not long-term borrowing.
services: Pawn loans secured by personal property (non-recourse)
Purchase and sale of used jewelry, electronics, tools, and appliances
Gold and precious metal buying for cash
Layaway purchase plans with 10% down payment
Retail merchandise inventory browsing and purchasing
Retail finance payment solutions (through AFF subsidiary)
